---
title: "RQ3 — Damage relative to area of control"
---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**In one line (3 of 3 research questions).** This page maps *where* 2026 cropland
damage falls relative to indicative areas of control. It is **descriptive only** and
makes **no** claim that either administration fared better, worse, or caused anything.
We deliberately present a table, not a comparison chart.
:::

::: {.conf-strip}
[Damage-vs-control framing]{.conf-item} [DESCRIPTIVE ONLY — NO COMPARISON]{.pill .pill-gap}
:::

::: {.callout-important}
**Descriptive only — no comparison is made ([DEC-005](glossary.qmd#dec-005)).** This is
a spatial overlay of 2026 cropland damage against *indicative* government-controlled
and former-AANES areas. Boundaries are contested and treated as indicative only; the
Euphrates split of Deir ez-Zor is a **schematic proxy line**, not an authoritative
control map. **No differential or causal claim is made or implied.** This layer maps
*where* damage falls, and nothing about either administration faring better or worse.
:::

**Question.** Descriptively, where does the validated 2026 cropland damage fall
relative to indicative areas of control?

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
import pandas as pd

REPO_ROOT = None
for _cand in (pathlib.Path.cwd(), *pathlib.Path.cwd().parents):
    if (_cand / "CLAUDE.md").exists() and (_cand / "viz").is_dir():
        REPO_ROOT = _cand
        sys.path.insert(0, str(_cand))
        break
assert REPO_ROOT is not None, "could not locate repo root (CLAUDE.md + viz/)"
RQ3 = REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "analysis" / "rq3_control_overlay"
```

## Why a table, and why no comparison — the responsible-analysis choice

This is the teaching moment of the study, so it is stated up front rather than buried.
A side-by-side bar chart of "damage under administration A versus B" would visually
**invite** the differential reading that [DEC-005](glossary.qmd#dec-005) forbids, even
with a caveat attached. So the page presents a **table, not a chart**, on purpose.

Two structural facts make a comparison impossible even if one were wanted:

1. **Deir ez-Zor spans both indicative zones**, and the per-AOI schema cannot
   apportion its hectares between them. An RQ analysis may not read the underlying
   rasters to do so (cross-reference discipline), so its hectares are reported
   **unapportioned**.
2. The national **fire-only governorates have no drawn control geometry** at all.
   Extending contested boundaries to force an overlay there would itself breach
   DEC-005.

## Descriptive tabulation — validated damaged cropland by indicative zone

```{python}
#| label: tbl-zones
#| echo: false
zones = pd.read_csv(RQ3 / "damage_by_indicative_zone.csv")
view = zones.rename(columns={
    "indicative_zone": "indicative zone", "aois": "AOIs",
    "flood_ha_headline_union": "flood ha", "fire_ha_headline_union": "fire ha",
    "total_ha_headline_union": "total ha",
})
(view.style.hide(axis="index")
     .format({"flood ha": "{:,.0f}", "fire ha": "{:,.0f}", "total ha": "{:,.0f}"}))
```

[↓ Zone tabulation (damage_by_indicative_zone.csv)](https://github.com/Muhanad-husn/flood-fire/blob/main/outputs/analysis/rq3_control_overlay/damage_by_indicative_zone.csv){.data-download}

*Hectares are the union-cropland headline; flood figures are the peak single
event-date extent (no cross-date double-count, [DEC-033](glossary.qmd#dec-033)).
Listing side by side is descriptive co-location, **not** a comparison.*

- **Deir ez-Zor** is reported as **spanning both** former-AANES (NE) and government
  (SW of the schematic Euphrates line), **unapportioned**, deliberately and not as an
  omission.
- The **national fire-only governorates** (Aleppo, Daraa, Hama, Homs, Idlib,
  Quneitra, Rural Damascus, Suwayda, Tartus) fall **outside** the drawn indicative
  control geometry; their burned cropland is reported for transparency with **no zone
  attributed**.

## Caveats (every artifact carries these)

- Indicative and contested boundaries, descriptive overlay only
  ([DEC-005](glossary.qmd#dec-005)). NOT an authoritative control map. No differential
  or causal claim may be drawn. The Euphrates is a schematic proxy line; control
  geography is the indicative 2017–2024 predominant pattern.
- Damaged hectares inherit the pipeline caveats: flood extent is open-water riverine
  inundation ([DEC-023](glossary.qmd#dec-023)); burned cropland is VIIRS-confirmed and
  a conservative lower bound ([DEC-031](glossary.qmd#dec-031)).
- **First-half-2026 scope ([DEC-039](glossary.qmd#dec-039)):** figures are lower
  bounds; the summer harvest and fire peak is unobserved.

::: {.page-nav}
[← RQ2 — Fire attribution](rq2.qmd)
[All three questions →](rq.qmd)
:::

{{< include _footer.qmd >}}
